tldr: Six of the model's named parameters tested. Q3 (gate τ) and Q6 (α) supported. Q2 (dose-response shape) supported qualitatively. Q1 (λ atrophy speed) bounded from below — calculator-analogue ruled out but exact magnitude not pinnable from current data. Q4 and Q5 untestable from current data — flagged as the load-bearing data gaps.

excluded_anchors.interpretation: Realized economy-level effect (≤2% on earnings) is roughly 1/10th of the median per-task α. The gap is the J-curve: per-task gains have not yet aggregated to measurable wage or hours effects.

dose_summary.note: psi_R re-fit downward from model default 0.003 to 0.0028 — within rounding of model default. The OpenAI-MIT data only constrains the qualitative shape; magnitudes still need raw-data refit.

lambda_summary.verdict: Bound from below only. The cross-sectional evidence rules out λ=0 (calculator-analogue) at p<.05 across multiple studies, but cannot pin λ above the lower bound. The 2+ year longitudinal study that would actually fit λ does not yet exist.

labor_results.ladder_break_evidence: Independent confirmation across two settings (US payroll panel + global freelance platform). Effect size larger in freelancer market (lower switching costs, more transparent pricing) consistent with freelancer market acting as the leading indicator.

aei_results.interpretation: Slow drift toward automation in consumer Claude.ai over 13 months (57% → 51% augmentation share, ~6pp). API surface dominated by automation throughout. Consistent with the model's claim that G3 (engagement-optimized substitution) is structurally favored over time, but slow.

verdicts.headline: Cross-sectional evidence rules out λ=0 (calculator-analogue) but cannot pin λ above the lower bound. Plausible λ at realistic offloading rates: 0.02–0.10/year. Model's default λ=0.06 sits in the middle of this range.
verdicts.load_bearing_data_gap: 2+ year longitudinal study with periodic capacity assessment. Bastani et al. 2025 is the closest existing prospective design (5-week deskilling RCT) but is not multi-year.

verdicts.headline: OpenAI-MIT N=981 supports the piecewise shape (low-dose protective, high-dose harmful, dose dominates modality). Magnitudes refit only to within a factor of ~2 — psi_R re-estimated at 0.0028 vs model default 0.003, beta_R unchanged at 0.001. d_safe ≈ 30 min is a useful pedagogical kink, not a structural claim about the curve.
verdicts.load_bearing_data_gap: Raw 300k-message dataset is gated. Without it, can confirm shape but not refit the slopes precisely. The catastrophic-loss mechanism (Replika ERP removal) is a separate failure mode the model does not encode.

verdicts.headline: BCG-Randazzo three-mode distribution implies τ between the self-automator centroid (f·ρ≈0.06) and the cyborg+centaur centroid (f·ρ≈0.445). Midpoint estimate: τ≈0.252. Model default τ=0.30 lands within 0.05 of the midpoint.
verdicts.load_bearing_data_gap: BCG individual-level f and ρ measurements are not directly published; f·ρ assignments here are inferred from the qualitative mode descriptions. Generalization beyond consulting is not tested.

verdicts.model_parameter: scalar T, B vs vector identity-domains
verdicts.headline: Would require multi-domain time-use survey paired with self-report identity-domain weights and AI-exposure measurement. ATUS gives domain time-use; no existing dataset combines all three.
verdicts.load_bearing_data_gap: New survey instrument needed. Cleanest design: existing ATUS respondents x identity-importance battery x AI-use frequency.

verdicts.headline: BPNSFS scale gives within-study coefficients but no portable population-level scale. SDT literature has not measured κ stratification by AI-exposure variation.
verdicts.load_bearing_data_gap: Cross-population BPNSFS panel with measured AI-exposure variation. Currently no such dataset exists at scale.

verdicts.headline: Across 8 positive-α anchors the spread is 4.2× (min 0.24 → max 1.01). Median α=0.45, mean α=0.53. Model default α=0.40 sits at the 37th percentile — a lower-middle anchor. Per-domain α should replace the scalar.
verdicts.load_bearing_data_gap: More within-study disaggregation by skill stock s. Many studies report headline effect without breaking down s, forcing imputation to recover α.
